metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4815
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4,5: subcellular fractions from rat striatum showed that PDE10A is localized to postsynaptic fractions, associates with membrane fractions but not the PSD fraction (like e.g. NMDA1 and PSD95). Authors conclude; "These results suggest that PDE10A associates with membranes through a non-ionic interaction."

Figure 7: Localization of PDE10A in rat striatum by immuno-electron microscopy. Particles were observed at the edge of PSDs but not directly apposed to these structures (and not in presynaptic terminals at all).. Experimental description: Figure 7: "Localization of PDE10A in rat striatum"

antibody:
PDE10A mouse monoclonal antibody 24F3.F11 (1:1000)
